prs.title: feat(ui): implement premium glassmorphism redesign and hero layout fixes
prs.body:
## Goal
Migrate the community website from a strict brutalist aesthetic to a modern, premium glassmorphic design to provide a stunning and highly dynamic first impression for developers and students. 

## Changes Made
* **Global Theme & Layout**:
  * Updated Tailwind configuration with vibrant crimson (`#ff2a4b`) gradients and deep dark backgrounds (`#050505`).
  * Replaced sharp corners (`0rem`) with modern rounded corners across all components.
  * Replaced solid red/black boxes with modern `.glass-panel` components utilizing `backdrop-blur(16px)` and subtle glowing borders.
* **Hero Section Enhancements**:
  * Added large, dynamic floating gradient blobs (`.blob`) to the background to make the page feel alive.
  * **Layout Fix**: Corrected the grid structure to properly group the call-to-action buttons into the left column beneath the text, and centered the hero image within the right column.
* **Chatbot UI Overhaul**:
  * Transformed the floating toggle button into a sleek, glowing circular icon with a smooth hover ripple.
  * Redesigned the chatbot modal into a glassmorphic panel featuring a deeply blurred backdrop, rounded corners, custom scrollbars, and styled chat bubbles with slide-up animations.
* **Interactive Elements**:
  * Softened the `particles.js` base colors and reduced connection opacity to elegantly match the new aesthetic without overwhelming the foreground content.
  * Updated the top navigation bar to feature a sleek `backdrop-blur-md` floating effect.

## Impact
These visual enhancements significantly elevate the premium feel of the DemonDie platform, ensuring it looks visually engaging, sophisticated, and developer-focused while maintaining great responsiveness across all devices.

prs.title: feat: UI Enhancements, Firebase Auth Integration, and Role-Based Blog Access
prs.body:
## Description
This PR cleans up the website UI by removing unused sections and improving layout alignments. It also introduces Firebase Authentication (Google & GitHub) and implements a role-based access control system for the "Add Blog" functionality, ensuring only authorized organization maintainers can publish content.

## Key Changes

### 🎨 UI & Layout Enhancements
- **Homepage Cleanup:** Removed the "Modules" section to allow the "Projects" section to utilize the full container width.
- **FAQ Page Fixes:** Removed the "OpenTicket" (Manual Override) section and fixed the padding/alignment issues within the FAQ answer dropdowns.
- **Custom Cursor:** Updated the custom cursor to be `64x64` and correctly aligned for precision clicking.
- **Navigation & Footer:** Removed the "About" link from the Navbar and the "Faculties" link from the Footer.
- **Contributor Wall:** Removed bot accounts and the "Join" button to streamline the display.

### 🔐 Authentication & Role-Based Access
- **Firebase Auth Integrated:** Added seamless Google and GitHub OAuth login capabilities.
- **Dynamic Blog Controls:** The "Add Blog" button is now strictly hidden by default. It dynamically appears only if the logged-in user is an authorized DemonDie Maintainer/Admin (`Pranav00076`, `Rishibyte`, `SharanyoBanerjee`, `Yuvraj`).
- **Robust GitHub Verification:** Improved the admin matching logic in `auth.js` to deeply inspect the GitHub OAuth payload and verify exact GitHub User IDs, ensuring reliable role assignments even if users have different display names.

## Testing Instructions
1. Navigate to `/index.html` and confirm the clean layout (no Modules section, clean Footer/Navbar).
2. Navigate to `/faqs.html` and verify the answer dropdown text alignment.
3. Navigate to `/blogs.html`. Ensure the "Add Blog" button is hidden.
4. Log in using GitHub or Google. If your account matches an authorized maintainer, the "Add Blog" button should appear dynamically.

## Deployment
Code is ready for production and has been tested locally and verified via Vercel deployments.

prs.title: refactor: unify visual theme, clean up comments, and implement cursor-swirling particles
prs.body:
Ran command: `git log -n 5 --oneline`

Here is a professional, markdown-formatted title and description for your Pull Request:

***

# Title
`refactor: unify visual theme, clean up comments, and implement cursor-swirling particles`

---

# Description

This Pull Request standardizes the styling and code hygiene across all subpages of the DemonDie website (`guidelines.html`, `404err.html`, `blogs.html`, `codeOfConduct.html`, `faqs.html`, and `licence.html`) to align perfectly with the main cyber-brutalist dashboard layout (`index.html`). It also integrates a dynamic interactive background particle canvas and custom cyberpunk cursors.

### Key Changes:

1. **Custom Interactive Cursors**:
   - Integrated custom cursor assets (`Cursor_64x64.png` for normal hover/movement, and `CursorActive_64x64.png` for active clicking state).
   - Implemented dynamic `<link rel="preload">` and JavaScript image caching preloads in `particles.js` to eliminate browser fetching latency and cursor flickering on click.
2. **High-Speed Canvas Particles (`particles.js`)**:
   - Added a sparse particle swarm (capped at a clean 175 particles for performance).
   - Set opacity to a high-contrast 80% (`rgba(255, 49, 49, 0.8)`) using a fast ambient motion vector.
   - Built orbit swirling physics that makes particles spin and wrap in a 300px threshold planetary ring around the active custom cursor.
3. **Visual & Styling Consistency**:
   - Corrected branding logo typography (`>_ DemonDie`) in all page navigation headers.
   - Resolved visual overrides by positioning `.crt-scanline` as an overlay, restoring correct black backgrounds and grid layout lines on `licence.html`.
   - Unified button glow styles to pure red (`#ff3131`) across guidelines, blogs, and code of conduct pages.
   - Standardized the `404err.html` terminal background with matched 40px grid backdrops.
4. **Link Cleanups**:
   - Corrected all references of `./liscence.html` typo to the correct path `./licence.html` in footer files.
   - Restored dead social links in the footer targeting Twitter and LinkedIn to point directly to official DemonDie handles.
5. **Codebase Refactoring & Quality**:
   - Standardized layout spacing, removed redundant/boiler-plate comments, and corrected syntax parsing rules in `fetch_recent_activity.js`, `fetch_community_feed.js`, and `chatbot.js`.
   - Verified JavaScript syntax across all files using `node -c` (zero parsing errors).

### Verification:
- All pages have been tested locally for formatting alignments.
- Custom cursor assets transition instantly without reverting to system defaults.
- Particles system maintains a locked 60 FPS profile.

prs.title: Updated with Real Data, AI Chatbot, Fixed Main Image.
prs.body:
Key Changes:

AI Chatbot (chatbot.js): Implemented a floating, toggleable chat assistant using the Hugging Face API (Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct). It dynamically references github_summary.json to answer queries about repositories and contributors, declining unrelated topics. Supports typing indicators and conversational memory.

Live Recent Activity (fetch_recent_activity.js): Replaced placeholder logs with a live feed of Demon-Die organization events (commits, PRs, stars).

Live Community Feed (fetch_community_feed.js): Fetches repository discussions via GraphQL, falling back to org-wide issues/PRs if unauthenticated. Obsolete static message box removed.

Env Configuration: Added .env token parsing to index.html with robust fallback options to support both server-hosted and local file:// environments.

issues.title: Refactor: Canvas Rendering Optimization for Large Boards
issues.body:
**Description:**
When the board exceeds 500+ shapes, panning and zooming might begin to drop frames.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Object Caching:** Set `fabric.Object.prototype.objectCaching = true`. This forces Fabric to render complex shapes to an offscreen canvas first.
2. **Render Throttling:** Ensure you aren't calling `canvas.renderAll()` excessively during Yjs updates. Wrap bulk updates in a single `requestAnimationFrame`.
3. **Viewport Culling:** (Advanced) Override the native rendering loop or selectively hide (`visible: false`) objects whose bounding boxes do not intersect with the current `canvas.vptCoords` bounding box to save GPU resources.

issues.title: Refactor: Offline Reconnection Grace Period
issues.body:
**Description:**
If a user temporarily loses WiFi, their local Yjs state might clash or miss updates when they reconnect.

**How to Implement:**
1. **IndexedDB Provider:** Install and configure `y-indexeddb`. Bind it to the same `Y.Doc` as your `y-websocket` or `y-webrtc` provider.
2. **Local Persistence:** This ensures all changes made offline are immediately written to local storage.
3. **Reconnection Sync:** When the WebSocket connection restores, Yjs will automatically compute the state vectors and sync the locally cached offline edits with the remote peers securely.

issues.title: Enhancement: Keyboard Shortcuts Modal
issues.body:
**Description:**
We have several keyboard shortcuts (V, H, R, C, T, Backspace), but no way for the user to discover them.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Modal UI:** Create a sleek dialog component (e.g., using Radix UI or native `<dialog>`) listing all available shortcuts and their actions.
2. **Trigger:** Add a small "?" or "Keyboard Shortcuts" button in the bottom right corner of the canvas. Also, allow opening the modal by pressing the `?` key (Shift + /).
3. **Content:** Document standard tools (V = Select, H = Hand/Pan, etc.) and global shortcuts (Delete/Backspace to delete, Ctrl+Z to undo, etc.).

issues.title: Enhancement: Dark / Light Mode Toggle
issues.body:
**Description:**
The application currently features a gorgeous hardcoded dark mode. However, a light mode would be highly appreciated for accessibility and daytime viewing.

**How to Implement:**
1. **CSS Variables / Tailwind:** If using Tailwind, use the `dark:` variant class. Otherwise, implement CSS variables for background and text colors.
2. **Toggle Button:** Add a sun/moon icon toggle in the dashboard and canvas UI.
3. **Canvas Background:** Dynamically update `canvas.backgroundColor` when the theme changes (e.g., from `#1e1e1e` to `#ffffff`) and call `canvas.renderAll()`. You may also need to invert default text colors.

issues.title: Bug: Mobile & Touch Support Optimization
issues.body:
**Description:**
The canvas does not behave perfectly on mobile devices. Trying to pan sometimes draws instead, and zooming is difficult.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Touch Events:** Ensure Fabric.js is interpreting touch events properly. You might need to explicitly include the Fabric.js touch module or gesture handlers.
2. **Panning:** Intercept touch events on the canvas container. If `touches.length === 2`, disable drawing mode and treat the motion as a pan gesture (`canvas.relativePan`).
3. **Zooming:** Calculate the distance between two touch points on `touchmove`. If the distance increases, zoom in; if it decreases, zoom out (`canvas.zoomToPoint`).
4. **UI Adjustments:** Hide hover-based tooltips and make toolbar buttons slightly larger for touch targets.

issues.title: Enhancement: Custom Fonts & Text Alignment
issues.body:
**Description:**
Text blocks and sticky notes only use the default font. We should allow basic text formatting.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Font Loading:** Import a few Google Fonts via CSS or `WebFontLoader`.
2. **Fabric Properties:** Update the `fontFamily` and `textAlign` properties on the selected `fabric.IText` or `fabric.Textbox` object.
3. **UI Updates:** Add a font-family dropdown menu and alignment toggle buttons (Left/Center/Right) to the text properties toolbar.
4. **Sync:** Add these properties to the Yjs sync payload for text objects.

issues.title: Enhancement: Multi-select Marquee Tool
issues.body:
**Description:**
Currently, the Select tool only selects one object at a time. Users expect to be able to click and drag a box to select multiple objects at once.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Enable Selection:** Ensure `canvas.selection = true` is set when the 'Select' tool is active. This enables the native Fabric.js drag-to-select box.
2. **Yjs Syncing:** Be careful with Yjs updates. When a multi-selection (an ActiveSelection group) is moved, Fabric emits modified events differently. Listen to `selection:created` and `selection:updated` to apply bulk coordinate translations to the underlying Yjs objects correctly.

issues.title: Feature: View-Only Mode
issues.body:
**Description:**
We need a way to share a room code with people who are only allowed to view the board, not edit it.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Room State:** When creating or joining a room, pass a role (e.g., `editor` vs `viewer`). This could be handled by different URL parameters or server-side logic.
2. **Fabric Constraints:** If the user is a viewer, set `canvas.selection = false`, disable drawing tools (`isDrawingMode = false`), and iterate through all objects setting `selectable: false` and `evented: false`.
3. **UI Constraints:** Hide the left toolbar and top properties bar for viewer roles.

issues.title: Feature: Export to PDF & SVG
issues.body:
**Description:**
We currently support exporting the canvas to a PNG. We should extend this to support vector formats (SVG) and PDFs for better print quality.

**How to Implement:**
1. **SVG Export:** Use Fabric's built-in `canvas.toSVG()` method. Create a Blob from the SVG string (`new Blob([svg], {type: 'image/svg+xml'})`) and trigger a download via a hidden `<a>` tag.
2. **PDF Export:** You can use a library like `jspdf`. Convert the canvas to an image (or parse the SVG) and insert it into a PDF document using `jspdf`'s API.
3. **UI Updates:** Change the export button to a dropdown menu with PNG, SVG, and PDF options.

issues.title: Feature: Object Locking
issues.body:
**Description:**
Important background elements (like grids or large template frames) are easily moved by mistake. Users should be able to lock objects in place.

**How to Implement:**
1. **UI Toggle:** Add a lock/unlock button to the properties floating menu when an object is selected.
2. **Fabric Properties:** Set `lockMovementX: true`, `lockMovementY: true`, `lockRotation: true`, `lockScalingX: true`, `lockScalingY: true`, and `hasControls: false` on the Fabric object.
3. **Yjs State:** Sync an `isLocked` boolean property via Yjs so other connected users also see the object as immovable.

issues.title: Feature: "Follow Me" Mode (Presentation Mode)
issues.body:
**Description:**
When presenting a board to a team, the presenter should be able to force all other users' viewports to lock onto theirs.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Yjs Awareness:** Update the presenter's awareness state to include a `presenting: true` flag and their current viewport coordinates (`canvas.vptCoords`).
2. **Client Sync:** Other clients listen to awareness changes. If another user is presenting, intercept their viewport changes.
3. **Panning:** Use `canvas.absolutePan()` and `canvas.setZoom()` on the viewers' screens to match the presenter's coordinates. Block user-initiated panning while following.

issues.title: Feature: Mini-map / Radar View
issues.body:
**Description:**
Because the canvas is infinite, it's easy to get lost or lose track of where other people are working. A small mini-map in the bottom corner would solve this.

**How to Implement:**
1. **Secondary Canvas:** Create a small secondary `canvas` element in the bottom corner.
2. **Rendering Map:** Instead of rendering the full Fabric canvas, render a simplified view (e.g., drawing standard rectangles representing the bounding boxes of all objects).
3. **Viewport Indicator:** Use the main canvas's `vptCoords` to draw a rectangle on the mini-map indicating the current view.
4. **Interactivity:** Add click listeners to the mini-map that call `canvas.absolutePan()` on the main canvas to jump to that area.

issues.title: Feature: Arrow / Line Connectors
issues.body:
**Description:**
To properly support flowcharting and mind-mapping, we need a dedicated tool to draw lines or arrows that connect two shapes. 

**How to Implement:**
1. **Drawing Lines:** Add a new tool to the toolbar. When active, mouse down starts a `fabric.Line`, and mouse move updates the line's `x2/y2` coordinates.
2. **Arrowheads:** You can either create a custom Fabric class extending line that draws a triangle at the end, or group a `fabric.Line` with a `fabric.Triangle`.
3. **Snapping (Optional):** When the line endpoints are near another shape's bounding box, automatically calculate the nearest edge center and anchor the line there. Add an `object:moving` listener to connected shapes to update the line's coordinates dynamically.

issues.title: Feature: Grouping and Ungrouping Objects
issues.body:
**Description:**
Users need the ability to group multiple shapes together so they can move, scale, and delete them as a single unit. 

**How to Implement:**
1. **Grouping:** When multiple objects are selected, provide a 'Group' button. On click, call `canvas.getActiveObject().toGroup()`.
2. **Ungrouping:** Provide an 'Ungroup' button when a group is selected. On click, call `canvas.getActiveObject().toActiveSelection()`.
3. **Yjs Synchronization:** You will need to represent the group in the Yjs state (e.g., nesting their IDs under a parent group ID) and handle the transformations of children relative to the group's transform.

issues.title: Feature: Image Upload & Paste Support
issues.body:
**Description:**
Currently, users can only draw shapes and text. It would be highly beneficial to allow users to upload images (PNG, JPG, SVG) via drag-and-drop or by pasting directly from their clipboard (Ctrl+V/Cmd+V).

**How to Implement:**
1. **Drag-and-Drop:** Add a `drop` event listener to the canvas container. Read the dropped file using `FileReader` or `URL.createObjectURL`.
2. **Clipboard Paste:** Add a `paste` event listener on the `window`. Check `event.clipboardData.items` for image types.
3. **Fabric.js Integration:** Use `fabric.Image.fromURL(url, (img) => { canvas.add(img) })`.
4. **Yjs Synchronization:** Store the image data (ideally as a base64 string or a blob reference if we add storage) into the Yjs map/array so it broadcasts to all peers.

issues.title: Performance optimization: lazy‑load images and improve LCP
issues.body: Implement lazy‑loading for heavy images and audit the site’s Largest Contentful Paint (LCP). **Good first issue**: Add `loading="lazy"` to existing `` tags. **Medium**: Use IntersectionObserver for background images. **Hard**: Reach an LCP under 1 second and document the steps.

issues.title: Add accessibility improvements (ARIA, keyboard navigation)
issues.body:
Improve the project’s accessibility by adding appropriate ARIA attributes, ensuring full keyboard navigation, and providing screen‑reader friendly descriptions.

**Good first issue**: Audit current pages and add ARIA labels to key interactive elements.

**Medium**: Implement focus‑visible outlines and test with a screen reader.

**Hard**: Achieve WCAG 2.2 AA compliance across the entire app.

issues.title: Improve project README with detailed setup guide
issues.body:
Add comprehensive setup instructions, environment variables, and screenshots to help newcomers get the project running locally.

**Good first issue**: Write the installation steps and add a screenshot of the running app.

**Medium**: Include a troubleshooting section for common errors.

**Hard**: Produce a short video walkthrough using GIFs.

issues.title: Add unit tests for core functionality
issues.body:
Write unit tests for the main website components, covering edge cases and ensuring reliability.

**Good first issue**: Add tests for the navigation bar rendering.

**Medium**: Add mock data tests for content loading.

**Hard**: Achieve 90% test coverage across the repo.

issues.title: Add documentation and UI enhancements for beginners
issues.body:
We are looking for first‑time contributors to help improve this project.

**Good first issue**: Write a README section that explains how to set up the site locally and add screenshots.

**Medium**: Update the UI styling (e.g., modern navigation bar with glassmorphism effect).

**Hard**: Optimize page load performance by implementing image lazy‑loading and reviewing Lighthouse scores.

Pick any task, open a PR, and follow the contribution guidelines.

issues.title: Implement lazy‑loading for canvas assets

**Good first issue**: Identify assets and add basic IntersectionObserver handling.

**Medium**: Add fallback for browsers without IntersectionObserver.

**Hard**: Measure performance impact and fine‑tune thresholds.

**Good first issue**: Draft the initial sections and add a checklist.

issues.title: Create Custom 404 Error Page
issues.body:
## 🚧 Create Custom 404 Error Page

### Description

Design and implement a custom 404 page to provide a better user experience when users navigate to a non-existent route.

### Requirements
- Friendly error message
- Consistent branding and styling
- Link/button to return to the homepage
- Responsive design

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Custom 404 page is displayed for invalid routes
- [ ] Page matches the application's design system
- [ ] Users can easily navigate back to the homepage
- [ ] Page is fully responsive

issues.title: Canvas Export / Download Menu
issues.body:
## 📥 Add Canvas Export Menu

### Description

Implement a Download menu in the top bar that allows users to export the current canvas in multiple formats.

### Requirements
- Export as PNG
- Export as SVG
- Export as PDF
- Utilize Fabric.js export capabilities

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Download menu is accessible from the top bar
- [ ] PNG export works correctly
- [ ] SVG export works correctly
- [ ] PDF export works correctly
- [ ] Exported files preserve canvas content accurately

issues.title: Sticky Note Tool
issues.body:
## 📝 Create Sticky Note Tool

### Description

Add a Sticky Note tool that allows users to place editable notes directly on the canvas.

### Requirements
- Draggable sticky note component
- Editable text content
- Predefined background color
- Resizable and selectable object support

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can create sticky notes from the toolbar
- [ ] Notes support text editing
- [ ] Notes can be moved around the canvas
- [ ] Notes integrate with existing canvas interactions

issues.title: Zoom Controls & Indicator
issues.body:
## 🔍 Add Zoom Controls

### Description

Add zoom controls in the bottom-right corner of the canvas, allowing users to precisely adjust the zoom level.

### Requirements
- Zoom percentage indicator
- Zoom in button
- Zoom out button
- Zoom slider
- Synchronization with canvas zoom state

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Current zoom level is displayed
- [ ] Zoom buttons function correctly
- [ ] Slider updates canvas zoom in real time
- [ ] Zoom state remains synchronized with the canvas

issues.title: Keyboard Shortcuts for Undo/Redo & Delete
issues.body:
## ⌨️ Add Keyboard Shortcuts

### Description

Implement global keyboard shortcuts for common canvas actions to improve productivity and user experience.

### Requirements
- `Ctrl + Z` → Undo last action
- `Ctrl + Shift + Z` → Redo last action
- `Delete` → Remove selected canvas object(s)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Undo shortcut triggers history undo
- [ ] Redo shortcut triggers history redo
- [ ] Delete key removes selected objects
- [ ] Shortcuts work consistently across the application

issues.title: Update Contributors Wall.
issues.body:
## Description

Currently, the Contributors Wall does not include all members of the organization.

### Expected Behavior
- Fetch all members associated with the organization.
- Display every organization member on the Contributors Wall.
- Ensure avatars, names, and profile links are shown correctly.
- Keep the Contributors Wall automatically updated when new members are added to the organization.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All organization members appear on the Contributors Wall.
- [ ] No duplicate entries are displayed.
- [ ] New members are reflected without manual updates.
- [ ] Existing contributor information remains accurate.

### Additional Notes
This enhancement will help properly recognize and showcase everyone contributing to the organization.
You may copy paste the structure, then change details.
